summary: This is a brief teaser post announcing an exploration of the Transformation Priority Premise applied to building a sort algorithm. Uncle Bob mentions using comic books as a pedagogical tool for teaching programming concepts. The post is essentially a stub pointing to external content created with Comic Life software. There is no substantive argument or conclusion presented in the text itself.
key_points: Transformation Priority Premise can be applied to building sort algorithms
Comic books can serve as pedagogical tools for teaching programming
The actual content appears to be in an external comic format rather than the blog post itself
money_quotes: We also explore comic books as a pedagogical tool.
key_insight: The Transformation Priority Premise, a TDD concept, can be taught through unconventional formats like comic books to make abstract programming principles more accessible.
